name: Spicy Stuffed Peppers
recipeIngredient: 2 teaspoons Chili powder, mild or hot (up to 3 teaspoons)
2 cups Cooked long-grain rice (white or brown)
recipeInstructions:
1. Ensure the peppers are well-shaped and will stand upright in a baking dish.
2. Carefully cut "lids" from the peppers and set aside.
3. Remove seeds and membranes, rinse with cold water, and place peppers in the baking dish.
4. In a large skillet, heat olive oil over medium-high heat. Add onion, beef, and walnuts; cook until beef is browned and onion is golden.
5. Add chili powder, cumin, and cook for 2 minutes, stirring constantly.
6. Stir in tomato sauce, raisins, olives, oregano, instant bouillon, and 1/2 cup water. Simmer for 5 minutes; remove from heat.
7. Stir in rice.
8. Preheat the oven to 400 degrees F (200 degrees C).
9. Spoon 1/4 of the rice mixture into each pepper and cover with reserved lids.
10. Pour 1/2 cup of water into the baking dish, cover tightly with foil, and bake for 50-60 minutes until peppers can be pierced easily with a knife.
11. Using a slotted spoon, remove peppers to a platter, garnish with parsley sprigs if desired, and serve.
Each serving (1 pepper) provides:
1-1/2 fat grams, 1/2 fiber gram, 5 vitamins, 1-1/2 proteins, 1 beta carotene
Per serving:
430 calories, 13 grams protein, 51 grams carbohydrates
20 grams fat: 6 grams polyunsaturated, 7 grams monounsaturated, 4 grams saturated
508 milligrams sodium, 27 milligrams cholesterol
